result.summary: Ory exposes a REST API for its identity/authz platform, an `ory` CLI, and a hosted docs MCP server; docs also state Ory Permissions supports gRPC APIs, but no separate public gRPC connection details were confirmed here.
result.description: Ory provides identity and access management products for authentication, OAuth2/OIDC, authorization, permissions, and related security use cases. It is available as a managed cloud service and as self-hosted open-source or enterprise deployments.

ory_project_api_key.label: Ory Network project API key
ory_project_api_key.setup: In the [Ory Console](https://console.ory.com/), open **Project settings → API Keys** for your project, click **Create new API key**, choose a name and expiration, create it, and copy the key immediately. The docs note the key is shown only once. Use this for privileged project admin API calls and project-data operations in the CLI. Keys typically have the `ory_apikey_` or `ory_pat_` prefix.

ory_workspace_api_key.label: Ory Network workspace API key
ory_workspace_api_key.setup: In the [Ory Console](https://console.ory.com/), open your workspace menu, go to **Workspace settings → API Keys**, create a workspace API key, and copy it when shown. Use this for privileged Ory Network workspace admin operations and workspace-scoped project configuration tasks in the CLI.

ory_cli_login.label: Ory CLI browser login session
ory_cli_login.setup: Install the `ory` CLI, then run `ory auth`. This opens a browser to sign in to your Ory account and stores CLI credentials locally for managing workspaces, projects, and project data. The docs state the CLI uses the OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow with Ory Hydra behind the scenes.

surfaces.name: Ory REST API

variables.name: project_slug
variables.description: The Ory Network project slug used as the subdomain, as shown in the API key usage example.

surfaces.notes: Ory documents that project frontend APIs do not require special authorization, but the public frontend endpoints are part of the same overall REST API surface while privileged admin operations require a project API key. Ory also states all services provide REST APIs, and Ory Permissions additionally supports gRPC APIs.
